title: Credit cards
description: Practice understanding of key credit card terms, select best credit card given specific scenario, and compare two credit cards.

assessment_items.question_content:
The Schumer box is a table that appears in credit card agreements.

**What is the main purpose of a Schumer box?**

[[☃ radio 2]]

choices.content: to show the benefits and rewards of using the card
to highlight the most important fees and interest rates of the card
to disclose the payment amount and due date of the card

assessment_items.question_content:
The Schumer box is a table that appears in credit card agreements.

**What specific information is required to be displayed in the Schumer box?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: APR, annual fees, late fees, grace period, and other penalties or fees
minimum credit score needed to qualify for the credit card

assessment_items.question_content:
The Schumer box is a table that appears in credit card agreements.  

**Who is required to provide the Schumer box?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: anyone who is applying for a credit card

assessment_items.question_content:
A Schumer box lists the main information about a credit card, such as its APR,  grace period, and minimum payment.

**What is meant by the term *grace period*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It is the amount of time you have to pay your bill in full before you are charged interest.
It is the amount of time you have to pay your annual fee.
It is the amount of time you have to dispute a transaction fee.

assessment_items.question_content:
A Schumer box lists the main information about a credit card, such as its APR,  grace period, and minimum payment.

**What is meant by the term *APR*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It is the yearly interest rate you will be charged on your credit card balance.
It is the amount of time you have to pay your annual fee.
It is the amount of time you have to pay off your balance before interest starts accruing

assessment_items.question_content:
A Schumer box lists the main information about a credit card, such as its APR,  grace period, and minimum payment.

**What is meant by the term *minimum payment*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It is the smallest amount you can pay each month to keep your account in good standing.
It is the amount you need to fully pay off your balance each month.
It is the annual fee associated with your credit card.

assessment_items.question_content:
Below is a Schumer box for a credit card.

Annual Percentage Rate (APR) | $22.99\%$
--- | ---
Grace period | $21$ days
Annual fee | $\$95$
Foreign transaction fee | $5\%$ of the purchase amount

Kim is considering signing up for this credit card, and her main concern is the cost of using her credit card while she is visiting her grandma in Belize.

**Which row of the Schumer box is relevant to Kim as she picks a credit card?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Below is a Schumer box for a credit card.

Annual Percentage Rate (APR) | $20.99\%$
--- | ---
Grace period | $30$ days
Annual fee | $\$55$
Foreign transaction fee | $3\%$ of the purchase amount

Sky is considering signing up for this credit card to have in case of an emergency, and their main concern are any fees associated with having the card. Sky does not travel internationally and does not plan on having a balance on the card.

**Which row of the Schumer box is relevant to Sky as they pick a credit card?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Below is a Schumer box for a credit card.

Annual Percentage Rate (APR) | $18.99\%$
--- | ---
Grace period | $30$ days
Annual fee | $\$65$
Foreign transaction fee | $1.5\%$ of the purchase amount

Santiago is considering signing up for this credit card to have in case of an emergency, and his main concern is how long he can wait to make a payment towards his balance.

**Which row of the Schumer box is relevant to Santiago as he picks a credit card?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Below is a Schumer box comparison of two credit cards.

 | Credit card A | Credit card B
--- | --- | ---
**Annual Percentage Rate (APR)** | $14.99\%$ | $19.99\%$
Grace period | $25$ days | $18$ days
Annual fee | $\$50$ | $\$0$
Late fee | $\$25$ | $\$30$
Foreign transaction fee | $\$0$ | $\$2.00$ per transaction 

Tya pays her credit card bills in full each month and does not like having to pay any additional costs for having a credit card. She does not travel outside of her country.

**Which credit card is the best choice for Tya?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Below is a Schumer box comparison of two credit cards.

 | Credit card A | Credit card B
--- | --- | ---
**Annual Percentage Rate (APR)** | $12.99\%$ | $16.99\%$
Grace period | $25$ days | $28$ days
Annual fee | $\$0$ | $\$0$
Late fee | $\$25$ | $\$30$
Foreign transaction fee | $\$0$ | $\$1.00$ per transaction 

Emily will be using this credit card to pay for her prescription glasses. She plans on paying it off in $3$ months and wants to pay the lowest amount of interest.  

**Which credit card is the best choice for Emily?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Below is a Schumer box comparison of two credit cards.

 | Credit card A | Credit card B|
--- | --- | --- |
**Annual Percentage Rate (APR)** | $16.99\%$ | $12.99\%$|
Grace period | $28$ days | $31$ days|
Balance transfer fee | $3\%$ of the amount transferred | $2\%$ of the amount transferred|
Late fee | $\$20$ | $\$30$|
Foreign transaction fee | $\$0$ | $\$1.00$ per transaction |

Emmanuel is looking to move the balance from his current card to a new credit card. His concern is the lowest cost for this type of transaction.

**Which credit card is the best choice for Emmanuel?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
